my_thoughts: The protocol works: think to best ability, compare results (when thinking gives results vs. when it doesn't), reject definition-seeking. The student is vulnerable (vulnerable helplessness), which shows they're seeking method. They'll likely ask about causal sequence next. Hypothesis: practical protocols trigger causal sequence questions—testable by tracking whether protocol responses lead to causal inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student admits incapacity ('I do not in the least know how to think not formatorily')—vulnerable helplessness via admits incapacity and requests instruction. They're seeking method/instruction. The comparison_contrast mechanism provides protocol: think to best ability, compare results (when thinking gives results vs. when it doesn't), reject definition-seeking. Pattern: incapacity admission → practical protocol → [next: causal question]. Medium confidence because practical protocols typically trigger clarification requests.
visible_response:
Think to the best of your ability and compare results—when your thinking gives
results and when it does not.
In that way you will come to a better understanding, at
any rate better than just wondering about it.
Definitions will not help you: desire for
definitions is only an excuse.
If you find yourself in a very difficult position, you will
think to the best of your ability how to get out of it.
Think in the same way.
